Thriller.name: Paul Bacon
Thriller.description: American book-jacket designer (1923–2015) — bold sans-serif typography over a flat coloured ground with a single emblematic icon or motif. Stark, graphic, type-driven. Examples: Catch-22, Jaws, Rosemary's Baby, Compulsion.

Science Fiction.name: Chesley Bonestell
Science Fiction.description: American astronomical illustrator (1888–1986) — photoreal painted vistas of planets, lunar landscapes, and rocket-age space scenes. Smooth airbrushed atmospheric perspective, dramatic celestial light, hard shadow.

Mystery.name: Tom Adams
Mystery.description: British surrealist illustrator (b. 1926) of Agatha Christie covers — hyperreal still lifes of fragmented objects (broken china, spilled tea, dolls' limbs) staged on plain backgrounds. Symbolic crime imagery without depicted violence.

Adventure.name: N.C. Wyeth
Adventure.description: American Golden Age book illustrator (1882–1945) — heroic figures in dramatic action, painterly oil with rich saturated colour and chiaroscuro. Treasure Island, Robin Hood, The Last of the Mohicans.

Comedy.name: Edward Gorey
Comedy.description: American author-illustrator (1925–2000) — meticulous crosshatched pen-and-ink drawings, monochrome or muted palette, Edwardian dress and oppressive interiors, deadpan macabre humour. Compositionally still.

Horror.name: Don Brautigam
Horror.description: American paperback illustrator (1946–2007) — atmospheric oil painting of empty interiors and exteriors at night, a single uncanny detail (a hand, a door ajar, a watching eye). Implied dread, no overt monster. Definitive Stephen King covers.

Fantasy.name: Michael Whelan
Fantasy.description: American fantasy and sci-fi cover artist (b. 1950) — lush photoreal oil painting, dramatic lighting, mythic figures and creatures rendered with documentary precision. Tolkien, Anne McCaffrey, Stephen King's Dark Tower.

Literary.name: Andrew Wyeth
Literary.description: American realist painter (1917–2009) — restrained tempera and watercolour, muted earth palette, cold pale light, lone figure or empty rural interior. Christina's World, Helga series. Psychological interiority through landscape.

Historical Fiction.name: Ilya Repin
Historical Fiction.description: Russian realist painter (1844–1930) — monumental historical and social tableaux, period dress and earthy palette, theatrical figuration with documentary weight. Barge Haulers on the Volga, Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks.

Myth & Folk Tale.name: Arthur Rackham
Myth & Folk Tale.description: British Golden Age illustrator (1867–1939) of fairy tales and Norse myth — sinuous Art Nouveau line, sepia and watercolour washes, gnarled trees and elvish figures, dream-logic landscapes.
